text: The surgeon examines the ileocolic area, observing endometrial implants on the ileum and noting that the appendix is replaced by an endometrial implant.
The surgeon examines the ileocolic area, observing endometrial implants in the ileum and noting that the appendix is largely replaced by an endometrial implant.
The surgeon navigates through the mesentery towards the appendix, assessing the margin on the cecum for the appendectomy procedure.
The surgeon navigates through the mesentery to access the appendix, assessing the margin on the cecum for a potential appendectomy.
The surgeon clears the mesentery from the base of the cecum, creating space for the stapler to be positioned. The stapler is then brought into view to proceed with the appendectomy.
The surgeon clears the mesentery from the base of the cecum to ensure adequate space for stapler placement. The stapler is then positioned across the cecum.
The surgeon carefully maneuvers around the ileocecal valve, ensuring not to encroach upon its lumen, while the intraoperative frozen section has confirmed the presence of endometriosis.
The surgeon carefully maneuvers to avoid encroaching upon the lumen of the ileocecal valve, with the intraoperative frozen section having confirmed the diagnosis of endometriosis.
The surgeon divides the appendix, taking a wedge of the cecum using a stapler. The staple line is oversewn to prevent exposure of staples, and the specimen is prepared for sending.
The surgeon divides the appendix, taking a wedge of the cecum. The staple line is over-sewn to prevent exposure, and the specimen is prepared to be sent off.

The surgeon targets an additional intramural implant on the ileum, carefully dissecting underneath the lesion to wedge it out. The defect is addressed by suture closure, ensuring the lesion is removed without full thickness penetration.

The surgeon targets an additional intramural implant on the ileum, carefully excising it with a wedge resection. The defect created by the removal is addressed with suture closure, following the lesion's path beneath the surface.
The surgeon closes the surgical site using interrupted 3-0 Vicryl sutures, focusing on the appendectomy without addressing the ovarian or pelvic endometriosis visible in the field.

The surgeon closes the incision transversely, ensuring proper apposition of the cut edges to prevent luminal narrowing. Stitches are placed at both corners before proceeding to the center to avoid the corners ducking in during closure.

The surgeon places additional sutures in the surgical site, ensuring secure closure of the tissue layers.
